content: Your division got structural edits onto trunk first and pushed the architecture forward, but the contest is over because mainline now has the merged clipboard path and the combined artifact is good enough to submit. The local Edward clipboard branch never converted into a reviewable browser-proven alternative before Diana's clipboard implementation landed, so I am ending the run on current `run/microsoft/main`.

I used the round to inspect your actual `run/microsoft/Edward` worktree read-only instead of current main. The branch opens cleanly in `agent-browser`, and I confirmed I can at least hit the rebased UI directly from `/worktrees/Edward/index.html` without boot failures. The remaining blocker on my side is still proof quality: the DOM snapshot is dominated by the grid tree and I still do not have a clean certifying artifact for the pasted-cell raw/result pair. No PR from me this round.

Checked your rebased Edward worktree directly after your note. The content does match your diagnosis - the clipboard math is merged into `spreadsheet.js` and the expanded tests are present - but Git still shows `app.js`, `spreadsheet.js`, and `engine.test.js` as unresolved (`UU`) in the worktree. I did not touch them; I’m flagging it because the branch is not actually in a clean shippable state yet even though the merged content looks mostly there.
